functions.name: all
functions.description:
Iterate over values of a table.

- If you want to iterate over keys, use `pairs(table)`.
 - If you want to iterate over index, use `ipairs(table)`.
 - If you want to iterate in reverse, use `rpairs(table)`.

calls.description: Iterate over the values of the table

functions.name: append
functions.description: Append *all values* from the table `source` to the table `dest`.

calls.description: Copy source into dest.

functions.name: merge
functions.description: Add *all key/value* from the table `source` to the table `dest`.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    local src = {x = 1, y = 2, z = 3}
    local dst = {a = 4, b = 5}
    local result = merge(src, dst)
    local index = 1
    for k,v in pairs(result) do
        print(k..":"..v , index * 4 * 8, 8)
        index = index + 1
    end
end

calls.description: Merge source into dest.

functions.name: new
functions.description: Create new instance of a class by creating a new table and setting the metatable. It allow to create kind of Object Oriented Programming.
functions.example:
local Player = {
    x = 128,
    y = 128,
    color = 8
}

function Player:draw()
    shape.rectf(self.x, self.y, 10, 10, self.color)
end

function _init()
    -- create a new player
    player = new(Player)
    -- create a new player with default vaules
    player2 = new(Player, {x = 200, y = 200, color = 9})
end

function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    player:draw() -- call the draw method on the player instance.
    player2:draw() -- call the draw method on the player2 instance.
end

calls.description: Create new instance of class.
Create new instance of class using default values.

functions.name: print
functions.description: Print on the screen a string. Default color is the closest to white (#FFFFFF) in the palette. To ensure visibility, use a color that contrasts with the cls() background color.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    -- every character is a sprite 4x4 pixels.
    print("hello")
    print("world", 10, 8)
    print("how", 10, 16, 4)
    print("are", 28, 16, 5)
    print("you", 46, 16, 6)
    print("...", 64, 16, math.rnd(10))
end

calls.description: print on the screen a string at (0,0) with the default color (closest to white).
print on the screen a string with the default color (closest to white).
print on the screen a string with a specific color index (1 to N).

functions.name: rpairs
functions.description: Iterate over values of a table in reverse order. The iterator return an index and the value. The method is useful to remove elements from a table while iterating on it.

calls.description: Iterate over the values of the table

libraries.name: console

functions.name: log
functions.description: Log a message into the console.
functions.example:
function _update()
    console.log("hello from the console")
    console.log("Log a value: ", tiny.frame)
    console.log("Log a table: ", ctrl.touch())
end
  
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    -- draw the mouse position.
    local pos = ctrl.touch()

    shape.line(pos.x - 2, pos.y, pos.x + 2, pos.y, 3)
    shape.line(pos.x, pos.y - 2, pos.x, pos.y + 2, 3) 
end

calls.description: Log a message into the console.

libraries.name: ctrl
libraries.description: Access to controllers like touch/mouse events or accessing which key is pressed by the user.

functions.name: pressed
functions.description: Return true if the key was pressed during the last frame. When called without argument, return a table of all keys pressed during the last frame (values matching the keys lib), or false if no key was pressed. If you need to check that the key is still pressed, see `ctrl.pressing` instead.
functions.example:
local percent_a = 1
local percent_b = 1

function _update()
    percent_a = math.min(percent_a + 0.05, 1)
    percent_b = math.min(percent_b + 0.05, 1)

    if ctrl.pressed(keys.space) then
        percent_a = 0
    end
    
    if ctrl.pressing(keys.space) then
        percent_b = 0
    end

    local offset_a = juice.powIn2(0, 8, percent_a)
    local offset_b = juice.powIn2(0, 8, percent_b)

    gfx.cls()
    shape.rectf(64, 128 - 16, 32, 32, 7)
    shape.rectf(64, 128 - 32 + offset_a, 32, 32, 8)

    shape.rectf(32 + 128, 128 - 16, 32, 32, 7)
    shape.rectf(32 + 128, 128 - 32 + offset_b, 32, 32, 8)

    print("pressed", 64, 128 + 32)
    print("pressing", 32 + 128, 128 + 32)
end

calls.description: Get all keys just pressed as a table of key values, or false if none.
Is the key was pressed?

functions.name: pressing
functions.description: Return true if the key is still pressed.
functions.example:
local percent_a = 1
local percent_b = 1

function _update()
    percent_a = math.min(percent_a + 0.05, 1)
    percent_b = math.min(percent_b + 0.05, 1)

    if ctrl.pressed(keys.space) then
        percent_a = 0
    end
    
    if ctrl.pressing(keys.space) then
        percent_b = 0
    end

    local offset_a = juice.powIn2(0, 8, percent_a)
    local offset_b = juice.powIn2(0, 8, percent_b)

    gfx.cls()
    shape.rectf(64, 128 - 16, 32, 32, 7)
    shape.rectf(64, 128 - 32 + offset_a, 32, 32, 8)

    shape.rectf(32 + 128, 128 - 16, 32, 32, 7)
    shape.rectf(32 + 128, 128 - 32 + offset_b, 32, 32, 8)

    print("pressed", 64, 128 + 32)
    print("pressing", 32 + 128, 128 + 32)

calls.description: Is the key is still pressed?

functions.name: touch
functions.description: Get coordinates of the current touch/mouse. If the mouse/touch is out-of the screen, the coordinates will be the last mouse position/touch. The function return those coordinates as a table {x, y}. A sprite can be draw directly on the mouse position by passing the sprite number.
functions.example:
function _draw()
  gfx.cls(2)
  p = ctrl.touch()
  print("coordinates: "..p.x .. "x"..p.y, 1, 1, 4)
  shape.rectf(p.x, p.y, 5,5, p.x + p.y)
end

calls.description: Get the mouse coordinates.
Get the mouse coordinate and draw a sprite on those coordinates.

functions.name: touched
functions.description:
Return the position of the touch (as `{x, y}`)if the screen was touched or the mouse button was pressed during the last frame. `nil` otherwise.
The touch can be : 

- 0: left click or one finger
- 1: right click or two fingers
- 2: middle click or three fingers

If you need to check that the touch/mouse button is still active, see `ctrl.touching` instead.
functions.example:
local circles = {}

function _update()
    local pos = ctrl.touched(0)
    if pos ~= nil then
        table.insert(circles, pos)
    end
end

function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    local p = ctrl.touch()
    shape.circlef(p.x, p.y, 4, 8)
    for pos in all(circles) do 
        shape.circlef(pos.x, pos.y, 4, 9)
        print("("..pos.x .. ", "..pos.y..")", pos.x + 3, pos.y + 3)
    end
end

calls.description: Is the screen was touched or mouse button was pressed?

functions.name: touching
functions.description:
Return the position of the touch (as `{x, y}`)if the screen is still touched or the mouse button is still pressed. `nil` otherwise.
The touch can be : 

- 0: left click or one finger
- 1: right click or two fingers
- 2: middle click or three fingers
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    local p = ctrl.touch()
    shape.circlef(p.x, p.y, 4, 8)

    local start = ctrl.touching(0)
    if start ~= nil then
        local pos = ctrl.touch()
        shape.line(start.x, start.y, pos.x, pos.y, 1)
        print("("..start.x .. ", "..start.y..")", start.x, start.y)
        print("("..pos.x .. ", "..pos.y..")", pos.x, pos.y)
    end
end

calls.description: Is the screen is still touched or mouse button is still pressed?

libraries.name: gfx
libraries.description: Access to graphical API like updating the color palette or applying a dithering pattern.

functions.name: camera
functions.description: Move the game camera.
functions.example:
local x = 0
local y = 0

function _update()
    if ctrl.pressing(keys.left) then
        x = x - 0.5
    elseif ctrl.pressing(keys.right) then
        x = x + 0.5
    end

    if ctrl.pressing(keys.up) then
        y = y - 0.5
    elseif ctrl.pressing(keys.down) then
        y = y + 0.5
    end
    gfx.camera(math.floor(x), math.floor(y))
end

function _draw()
    gfx.cls(2)
    for x = 0 - 64, 256 + 64, 16 do
        for y = 0 - 64, 256 + 64, 16 do
            shape.line(x - 2, y, x + 2, y, 9)
            shape.line(x, y - 2, x, y + 2, 9)
        end
    end
    print("camera: ("..x..", "..y..")", 6, 6)
    
    shape.rect(0, 0, 256, 256, 1)
end

calls.description: Reset the game camera to it's default position (0,0).
Set game camera to the position x, y.

functions.name: clip
functions.description: Clip the draw surface (ie: limit the drawing area).
functions.example:
function _init()
  c = {}
  for i=1,100 do
    table.insert(c, {x=math.rnd(256), y=math.rnd(256), c = math.rnd(1,12)})
end

end
function _draw()
  gfx.cls()
  local pos = ctrl.touch()
  -- set a clip area to crop circles
  gfx.clip(pos.x - 20, pos.y - 20, 40, 40)
  for circle in all(c) do
    shape.circlef(circle.x, circle.y, 10, circle.c)
  end
end

calls.description: Reset the clip and draw on the fullscreen.
Clip and limit the drawing area.

functions.name: cls
functions.description: Clear the screen. When called without arguments, clears with the color closest to black (#000000) in the palette. To ensure visibility, always draw with a color index DIFFERENT from the cls() color.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    if ctrl.pressed(keys.space) then
       gfx.cls()
    end

    print("Press space to clear the screen") 
    local pos = ctrl.touch()
    shape.circlef(pos.x, pos.y, 4, math.rnd())
end

calls.description: Clear the screen with the color closest to black (#000000) in the palette.
Clear the screen with the given color index (1 to N). Color 0 clears to transparent.

functions.name: dither
functions.description: Apply a dithering pattern on every new draw call. The pattern is using the bits value of a 2 octet value. The first bits is the one on the far left and represent the pixel of the top left of a 4x4 matrix. The last bit is the pixel from the bottom right of this matrix.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    gfx.dither()
    shape.circlef(30, 30, 30, 2)
    gfx.dither(0xA5A5) -- set a dithering pattern
    shape.circlef(50, 50, 30, 3)
    gfx.dither(0x0842) -- set another dithering pattern
    shape.circlef(70, 70, 30, 2)
end

calls.description: Reset dithering pattern. The previous dithering pattern is returned.
Apply dithering pattern. The previous dithering pattern is returned.

args.name: pattern
args.description: Dither pattern. For example: 0xA5A5 or 0x3030

functions.name: draw_mode
functions.description:
Switch to another draw mode. 

- 0: default.
 - 1: drawing with transparent (ie: can erase part of the screen)
  - 2: drawing a stencil that will be use with the next mode
  - 3: drawing using a stencil test (ie: drawing only in the stencil)
  - 4: drawing using a stencil test (ie: drawing everywhere except in the stencil)
functions.example:
local draw_stencil = 2
local draw_inside_stencil = 3
local draw_outside_stencil = 4

local outside = false

function _draw()
    if ctrl.pressed(keys.space) then
        outside = not outside
    end

    gfx.cls(3)

    gfx.draw_mode(draw_stencil)

    -- draw a circle in the middle of the screen
    shape.circlef(128, 128, 64, 1)
    
    if outside then
        gfx.draw_mode(draw_inside_stencil)
    else
        gfx.draw_mode(draw_outside_stencil)
    end
    
    -- draw the sprite sheet. only in the circle
    spr.sdraw()
    gfx.draw_mode()
end

calls.description: Return the actual mode. Switch back to the default mode.
Switch to another draw mode. Return the previous mode.

functions.name: pal
functions.description: Change a color from the palette to another color.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    print("example", 10, 10, 2) -- print using the color index 2
    gfx.pal(2, 3) -- switch the text color to another color
    print("example", 10, 20, 2) -- print using the color index 2
    gfx.pal() -- reset the palette
    print("example", 10, 30, 2) -- print using the color index 2
end

calls.description: Reset all previous color changes.
Replace the color a for the color b.

functions.name: pget
functions.description: Get the color index at the coordinate (x,y).
functions.example:
function _draw()
   gfx.cls()
   local index = 0
   for x=0, 240, 16 do
     for y=0, 240, 16 do
        shape.rectf(x, y, 16, 16, index)
        index = index + 1
     end
   end

   local pos = ctrl.touch()
   local color = gfx.pget(pos.x, pos.y)
   if color ~= nil then 
     shape.rectf(0, 0, 80, 6, 13)
     print("color index: "..color)
   end


   shape.circlef(pos.x - 2, pos.y - 2, 4, 0)
end

calls.description: get the color index at the coordinate (x,y).

functions.name: pset
functions.description: Set the color index at the coordinate (x,y).
functions.example:
function _draw()
   local pos = ctrl.touching(0)
   if pos ~= nil then
      -- set the pixel with the color 9 when the mouse is pressed
      local p = ctrl.touch()
      gfx.pset(p.x, p.y, 9)
   end
end

calls.description: set the color index at the coordinate (x,y).

functions.name: to_sheet
functions.description:
Transform the current frame buffer into a spritesheeet. 

- If the index of the spritesheet already exist, the spritesheet will be replaced
- If the index of the spritesheet doesn't exist, a new spritesheet at this index will be created
- If the index of the spritesheet is negative, a new spritesheet will be created at the last positive index.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls(1)
    -- draw a transparent circle (like a hole)
    shape.circlef(64, 128, 20, 0)
    -- keep the result as spritesheet 0
    gfx.to_sheet(0)

    gfx.cls(1)
    -- draw some circles
    shape.circlef(64, 108, 20, 8)
    shape.circlef(44, 128, 20, 9)
    shape.circlef(64, 148, 20, 10)
    shape.circlef(84, 128, 20, 11)

    -- draw over the circles
    -- the mask generated before.
    spr.sheet(0)
    spr.sdraw()
end

calls.description: Copy the current frame buffer to an new or existing sheet index.

libraries.name: juice
libraries.description:
Easing functions to 'juice' a game. Interpolation to juice your game.
All interpolations available: 

- pow2, pow3, pow4, pow5,
- powIn2, powIn3, powIn4, powIn5,
- powOut2, powOut3, powOut4, powOut5,
- sine, sineIn, sineOut,
- circle, circleIn, circleOut,
- elastic, elasticIn, elasticOut,
- swing, swingIn, swingOut,
- bounce, bounceIn, bounceOut,
- exp10, expIn10, expOut10,
- exp5, expIn5, expOut5,
- linear

functions.name: bounce
functions.example:
local center_x = 256 * 0.5
local center_y = 256 * 0.5
local width = 128

function _update()
    gfx.cls()
    shape.line(center_x - 64, center_y + 64, center_x + 64, center_y + 64, 2)
    shape.line(center_x + 64, center_y - 64, center_x + 64, center_y + 64, 2)

    
    for x = 0, width, 2 do
        local y = juice.##function##(0, 128, x / width)
        gfx.pset(
            center_x - 64 + x, center_y + 64 - y, 3
        )
    end

    local percent = (tiny.frame % 100) / 100
    local x = width * percent
    local y = juice.##function##(0, 128, percent)
    shape.circlef(center_x - 64 + x, center_y + 64 - y, 4, 7)
    shape.rectf(center_x - 64 + x - 2, center_y + 64 + 8, 4, 4, 7)
    shape.rectf(center_x + 70, center_y + 64 - y, 4, 4, 7)
    local name = "##function##"
    print(name, center_x - #name * 4 * 0.5, center_y + 92)
end

calls.description: Give a percentage (progress) of the interpolation
Interpolate the value given a start and an end value.

args.name: progress
args.description: Progress value. Needs to be between 0 (start of the interpolation) and 1 (end of the interpolation)

functions.example:
local center_x = 256 * 0.5
local center_y = 256 * 0.5
local width = 128

function _update()
    gfx.cls()
    shape.line(center_x - 64, center_y + 64, center_x + 64, center_y + 64, 2)
    shape.line(center_x + 64, center_y - 64, center_x + 64, center_y + 64, 2)

    
    for x = 0, width, 2 do
        local y = juice.##function##(0, 128, x / width)
        gfx.pset(
            center_x - 64 + x, center_y + 64 - y, 3
        )
    end

    local percent = (tiny.frame % 100) / 100
    local x = width * percent
    local y = juice.##function##(0, 128, percent)
    shape.circlef(center_x - 64 + x, center_y + 64 - y, 4, 7)
    shape.rectf(center_x - 64 + x - 2, center_y + 64 + 8, 4, 4, 7)
    shape.rectf(center_x + 70, center_y + 64 - y, 4, 4, 7)
    local name = "##function##"
    print(name, center_x - #name * 4 * 0.5, center_y + 92)
end

functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    
    local pos = ctrl.touch()

    local x = math.clamp(60, pos.x, 256 - 60)
    
    gfx.dither(0xA5A5)
    shape.line(64, 129, 256 - 60, 129, 9)
    gfx.dither()
    shape.rect(60, 128, 4, 4, 9)
    shape.rect(256 - 60, 128, 4, 4, 9)
    shape.circlef(pos.x, pos.y, 2, 9)
    shape.circle(x, 129, 2, 8)
end

functions.name: dst
functions.description: Compute the distance between two points.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    
    local pos = ctrl.touch()
    shape.line(pos.x, pos.y, 128, 128, 3)
    shape.circlef(128, 128, 2, 9)
    shape.circlef(pos.x, pos.y, 2, 9)

    -- midle of the line
    local x = (pos.x - 128) * 0.5
    local y = (pos.y - 128) * 0.5
    
    -- display dst
    local dst = math.dst(128, 128, pos.x, pos.y)
    print("dst: "..dst, 128 + x, 128 + y)
end

calls.description: Distance between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2).

functions.name: dst2
functions.description: Compute the distance between two points not squared. Use this method to know if an coordinate is closer than another.
functions.example:
local a = {
    x = 10 + math.rnd(236),
    y = 10 + math.rnd(236)
}

local b = {
    x = 10 + math.rnd(236),
    y = 10 + math.rnd(236)
}

function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    
    local pos = ctrl.touch()

    if math.dst2(pos.x, pos.y, a.x, a.y) > math.dst2(pos.x, pos.y, b.x, b.y) then
        -- b is closer
        shape.line(pos.x, pos.y, b.x, b.y, 3)
    else
        -- a is closer
        shape.line(pos.x, pos.y, a.x, a.y, 3)
    end
    shape.circlef(a.x, a.y, 2, 9)
    shape.circlef(b.x, b.y, 2, 9)
    shape.circlef(pos.x, pos.y, 2, 9)
end

calls.description: Distance not squared between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2).

functions.name: triangle
functions.description: Draw a triangle using the coordinates of (x1, y1), (x2, y2) and (x3, y3) and color.
functions.example:
function tri(f, fill)
  local x1 = 128 + math.cos(f) * 64
  local y1 = 128 + math.sin(f) * 64

  local x2 = 128 + math.cos(f + math.pi * 1/3) * 64
  local y2 = 128 + math.sin(f + math.pi * 1/3) * 64

  local x3 = 128 + math.cos(f+ math.pi * 2/3) * 64
  local y3 = 128 + math.sin(f +math.pi * 2/3) * 64

  if fill then
    shape.trianglef(x3, y3, x2, y2, x1, y1, 8)
  else
    shape.triangle(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3, 8)
  end
end

function _draw()
  gfx.cls()
  local f = tiny.frame * 0.01

  tri(f, false)
  tri(f*2, true)
end

functions.name: trianglef
functions.description: Draw a filled triangle using the coordinates of (x1, y1), (x2, y2) and (x3, y3) and color.
functions.example:
function tri(f, fill)
  local x1 = 128 + math.cos(f) * 64
  local y1 = 128 + math.sin(f) * 64

  local x2 = 128 + math.cos(f + math.pi * 1/3) * 64
  local y2 = 128 + math.sin(f + math.pi * 1/3) * 64

  local x3 = 128 + math.cos(f+ math.pi * 2/3) * 64
  local y3 = 128 + math.sin(f +math.pi * 2/3) * 64

  if fill then
    shape.trianglef(x3, y3, x2, y2, x1, y1, 8)
  else
    shape.triangle(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3, 8)
  end
end

function _draw()
  gfx.cls()
  local f = tiny.frame * 0.01

  tri(f, false)
  tri(f*2, true)
end

libraries.name: sound
libraries.description:
Sound API to play/loop/stop a sound.
A sound can be created using the sound editor, using the command line `tiny-cli sfx <filename>`.

WARNING: Because of browser behaviour, a sound can *only* be played only after the first
user interaction.

Avoid to start a music or a sound at the beginning of the game.
Before it, force the player to hit a key or click by adding an interactive menu
or by starting the sound as soon as the player is moving.

functions.name: music
functions.description: Play a music

calls.description: Play the music at the index music_index. The music can be looped.

functions.name: note
functions.description: Play a note by an instrument until it's stopped
functions.example:
function _init()
    keys = {
        {note="E4", x=57, y=100, w=16, h=50},
        {note="Fs4", x=75, y=100, w=16, h=50},
        {note="Gs4", x=93, y=100, w=16, h=50},
        {note="A4", x=111, y=100, w=16, h=50},
        {note="B4", x=129, y=100, w=16, h=50},
        {note="Cs5", x=147, y=100, w=16, h=50},
        {note="Ds5", x=165, y=100, w=16, h=50},
        {note="E5", x=183, y=100, w=16, h=50}
    }
    active_notes = {}
end

function _update()
    local touch = ctrl.touching(0)

    for i, key in ipairs(keys) do
        if in_bounds(touch, key) then
            active_notes[i] = active_notes[i] or sound.note(key.note, 1)
        else
            active_notes[i] = active_notes[i] and active_notes[i].stop()
        end
    end
end

function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    print("E MAJOR SCALE", 57, 84, 15)
    print("Click keys to play", 57, 92, 14)

    for i, key in ipairs(keys) do
        if active_notes[i] then
            shape.rectf(key.x, key.y, key.w, key.h, 15)
        else
            shape.rect(key.x, key.y, key.w, key.h, 14)
        end
    end
    local pos = ctrl.touch()
    shape.circlef(pos.x, pos.y, 2, 2)
end

function in_bounds(touch, key)
    return touch and touch.x >= key.x and touch.x < key.x + key.w and
           touch.y >= key.y and touch.y < key.y + key.h
end

libraries.name: spr
libraries.description: Sprite API to draw or update sprites.

functions.name: draw
functions.description: Draw a sprite.
functions.example:
function _init()
  id = 1
end

function _draw()
    if ctrl.pressed(keys.left) then
      id = id - 1
    elseif ctrl.pressed(keys.right) then
      id = id + 1
    end

    gfx.cls()
    print("sprite index "..id.. " (press left or right to change)", 50, 112)
    spr.draw(id, 120, 120)
end

functions.name: pget
functions.description: Get the color index at the coordinate (x,y) from the current spritesheet.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    local pos = ctrl.touch()
    
    gfx.cls()
    
    spr.sdraw()
    shape.circlef(pos.x - 4, pos.y - 4, 8, 3)
    local color = spr.pget(pos.x, pos.y)
    if(color ~= nil) then
        print("index color: "..color, 7, 0)
        shape.rectf(0, 0, 6, 6, color)
        shape.circlef(pos.x - 4, pos.y - 4, 6, color)
    end
end

functions.name: pset

functions.example:
function _draw()
    local pos = ctrl.touch()

    local touching = ctrl.touching(0)
    gfx.cls()

   spr.sdraw()

   if touching ~= nil then
      spr.pset(pos.x, pos.y, 9)
   end
   print("click to alter", 45, 96)
   shape.circle(64 + 8, 128 + 8, 32, 1)
   shape.circlef(128 + 8, 128 + 8, 32, 1)
   spr.draw(100, 128, 128)

   shape.circlef(pos.x, pos.y, 2, 3)
end

functions.name: sheet
functions.description: Switch to another spritesheet. The index of the spritesheet is given by it's position in the spritesheets field from the `_tiny.json` file.The first spritesheet is at the index 0. It retuns the previous spritesheet. The spritesheet can also be referenced by its filename.
functions.example:
local current = 0
function _update()
    local x = math.perlin((tiny.frame % 100) / 100, (tiny.frame % 100) / 100, (tiny.frame % 100) / 100)
    local y = math.perlin((tiny.frame  * 0.5 % 100) / 100, (tiny.frame % 100) / 100, (tiny.frame * 0.5 % 100) / 100)

    gfx.cls()
    shape.circlef(x * 256, y * 256, 10, 8)
    gfx.to_sheet("circle.png")
    
    gfx.cls()
    shape.rectf(x * 256, y * 256, 20, 20, 8)
    gfx.to_sheet("rect.png")

    if ctrl.pressed(keys.space) then
        current = (current + 1) % 2
    end
end

function _draw()
    if current == 0 then
        spr.sheet("circle.png")
    else
        spr.sheet("rect.png")
    end
    spr.sdraw()
end

libraries.name: text
libraries.description: Text rendering library for custom fonts. Allows selecting fonts configured in `_tiny.json` and rendering text with them. When no font is selected, uses the default boot font (same as `print()`).

functions.name: font
functions.description: Select a font to use for text rendering. Call without arguments to reset to the default boot font.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    -- Use the default boot font
    text.font()
    text.print("default font", 10, 10)

    -- Switch to the first custom font (index 0)
    text.font(0)
    text.print("custom font", 10, 30)

    -- Switch to a custom font by name
    text.font("big_font")
    text.print("big font", 10, 50)

    -- Reset to default
    text.font()
    text.print("back to default", 10, 70)
end

calls.description: Reset to the default boot font.
Select a font by index.

functions.name: print
functions.description: Print text on the screen using the currently selected font.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    -- Print with default color (white)
    text.print("hello world", 10, 10)

    -- Print with a color index
    text.print("colored text", 10, 20, 9)

    -- Print multiline text
    text.print("line 1\nline 2\nline 3", 10, 40)
end

calls.description: Print text at the given position with an optional color.

functions.name: width
functions.description: Measure the width in pixels of a string using the currently selected font.
functions.example:
function _draw()
    gfx.cls()
    local msg = "hello"
    local w = text.width(msg)
    -- Center the text on screen
    local x = (256 - w) / 2
    text.print(msg, x, 120)
end

calls.description: Returns the width in pixels of the text.

functions.name: dot
functions.description: Dot product between two vectors
functions.example:
function _init()
    angle = 0
end
function _update()

    if ctrl.pressing(keys.space) then
        angle = angle + 0.1
    end

    gfx.camera(-128, -128)

    local v1 = vec2.create(1, 0)
    local v2 = vec2.create(math.cos(angle), math.sin(angle))
    local dot = vec2.dot(v1, v2)
    
    local scl = vec2.scl(v1, dot)
    
    gfx.cls(1)
    
    local scaledv1 = vec2.scl(v1, 64)
    local scaledv2 = vec2.scl(v2, 64)
    local scaledv3 = vec2.scl(scl, 64)

    print("v1", 18, 5, 10)
    shape.line(0, 0, scaledv1.x, scaledv1.y, 10)
    print("v2", scaledv2.x + 5, scaledv2.y + 5, 11)
    shape.line(0, 0, scaledv2.x, scaledv2.y, 11)
    
    print("dot", scaledv3.x, 5, 9)
    shape.line(0, 0, scaledv3.x, scaledv3.y, 9)
end
